performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: This folder holds the proposals written in one language. Keep in variants[] only the proposals in THIS folder's language; move the others to crossReferences (their own language folders).

variants.languageReason: Why this language gives the best fast-and-lean default for this problem. Do not cite a repository-wide language requirement.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Considered for native runtime, contiguous storage, and platform ergonomics; explain whether it wins or why another language beats it.
Considered for minimal runtime overhead and memory control; explain whether the lower-level API helps or is outweighed by platform/signature costs.
Considered for native performance and memory safety; explain whether platform support and implementation overhead fit this problem.
Considered for compiled performance and simple data structures; explain whether GC/runtime overhead or platform support changes the trade-off.
Considered when managed runtimes are supported and competitive; explain whether JIT, GC, object layout, or library ergonomics help or hurt.
Considered when constraints are small, built-ins dominate, or platform scoring favors them; explain why interpreter/VM overhead is acceptable or not.

variants.languageReason: Why this language wins raw/asymptotic runtime for this problem.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Considered for raw native speed, inlining, and optimized standard-library primitives.
Considered for minimal abstraction overhead and manual memory layout.
Considered for native speed with strict memory guarantees; note platform and implementation-cost trade-offs.
Considered for compiled loops and straightforward implementation; note GC/runtime and bounds-check trade-offs.
Considered for strong JIT performance where platform startup and allocation patterns are acceptable.
Considered only when built-ins, small constraints, or platform behavior can overcome VM/interpreter overhead.

variants.languageReason: Why this language minimizes the memory footprint for this problem.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Considered for compact contiguous containers and stack storage; explain any unavoidable container/signature overhead.
Considered for the smallest manual layouts and no container/runtime overhead.
Considered for native memory control with safety checks; explain whether platform support and code shape remain lean.
Considered for simple slices and maps; explain whether runtime/GC overhead is too large for the memory objective.
Considered when managed allocation is still compact enough; explain object/header/GC trade-offs.
Considered only when input sizes are tiny or built-in representations avoid custom auxiliary structures.
